description_long: This building is a large, modern museum complex composed of several interconnected structures with distinct geometric forms. The central feature is a cylindrical glass-walled rotunda, which is flanked by rectangular and curved wings, suggesting a mix of gallery and administrative spaces. The left wing is notably curved, while the right wing features a long, rectangular hall with a series of evenly spaced vertical elements, possibly skylights or clerestory windows. The exterior surfaces are predominantly light gray, with the central rotunda displaying transparent or translucent glass panels. The overall design is open and inviting, with multiple entry points and a layout that encourages circulation around the central hub. The structure appears to be constructed primarily from concrete, glass, and steel, reflecting a contemporary architectural style.
description: A modern museum complex with a central glass rotunda, flanked by curved and rectangular wings in light gray and glass materials.
description_view_0: The front view highlights the cylindrical glass rotunda at the center, with the curved left wing and rectangular right wing clearly visible.
description_view_1: This angle emphasizes the long rectangular wing with vertical elements and provides a clear view of the rear rectangular block.
description_view_2: The view shows the back of the museum, with the curved wing on the right and the rectangular wings extending outward from the central rotunda.
description_view_3: The perspective focuses on the broad, curved facade and the glass rotunda, with the rectangular wings receding into the background.
